title: Challenge Lab - Configuring Layered Security in an AWS VPC

tasks.title: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Open the AWS Management Console in a new browser tab via Open Console.
Retain the default Account ID, enter the provided IAM Username and Password, and sign in.
Select us-east-1 (N. Virginia) from the top-right region dropdown.
Sign in to AWS Management Console

XYZ Company needs a secure AWS VPC with layered security using Terraform. Your job is to set up a VPC with public and private subnets, route tables, an internet gateway, security groups, network ACLs, and EC2 instances, then test and validate the setup. Here’s how to proceed:
Create and Attach an Internet Gateway
Create Two Subnets(1 public, 1 private)
Create Route Tables, Configure Routes, and Associate Them with Subnets
Create a public route table: Associate with IGW,PublicSubnet.
Associate with IGW,PublicSubnet.
Create a private route table: Associate with PrivateSubnet (no internet route).
Associate with PrivateSubnet (no internet route).
Add inbound rules: SSH (port 22) from 0.0.0.0/0. All ICMP – IPv4 ports from 0.0.0.0/0.
SSH (port 22) from 0.0.0.0/0.
All ICMP – IPv4 ports from 0.0.0.0/0.
Allow all outbound traffic.
Select VPC.
Create and Add rules: Inbound rules : Add rules for SSH (port 22) and All ICMP IPv4 Outbound: Add rules for Custom TCP Rule (port 1024 - 65535) and All ICMP IPv4
Inbound rules : Add rules for SSH (port 22) and All ICMP IPv4
Outbound: Add rules for Custom TCP Rule (port 1024 - 65535) and All ICMP IPv4
Associate the NACL with PublicSubnet and PrivateSubnet.
Region: us-east-1:
AMI: Amazon Linux 2023 AMI

INSTANCE TYPE: t2.micro . Launch one in PublicSubnet1 (name: PublicEC2): Enable Auto-assign Public IP. Launch one in PrivateSubnet1 (name: PrivateEC2): Disable public IP. Assign previously created SG. Keypair
Launch one in PublicSubnet1 (name: PublicEC2): Enable Auto-assign Public IP.
Enable Auto-assign Public IP.
Launch one in PrivateSubnet1 (name: PrivateEC2): Disable public IP.
Disable public IP.
Assign previously created SG.
Keypair
SSH into PublicEC2 using its public IP .
Verify connectivity (e.g., ping google.com).
Ping the private IP of your private_instance
Make sure everything is correct. In the lab console, click Validate to check if both CloudFormation stacks created the VPCs and subnets correctly.
Sign out of the AWS Management Console by clicking your username (top-right) and selecting Sign out.
In the lab console, click End Challenge and wait for it to complete.
You’ve completed the challenge!
